full_prompt: Design a single-part, monolithic combustion chamber (case) for a student-researched hybrid rocket motor intended for 30 km (30,000 ft) apogee. Oxidizer is nitrous oxide (N2O); fuel grain is HTPB. The chamber is a seamless, spin-formed or machined monolithic cylinder with two integral bosses: a forward injector-bulkhead interface (8x M6 bolt pattern on a 120 mm PCD) and an aft nozzle-retention interface (12x M8 bolt pattern on a 140 mm PCD). Internal diameter 140 mm, internal length 700 mm, wall thickness to be sized. Material is 6061-T6 aluminum with derating for hot-fire temperature 150 C (ablative liner protects the wall): Young's modulus 65 GPa at 150 C, yield strength 240 MPa at 150 C, ultimate strength 276 MPa at 150 C, density 2700 kg/m^3. Maximum Expected Operating Pressure (MEOP) is 60 bar (6 MPa) chamber pressure with a transient pressure spike allowance of 1.1 x MEOP = 66 bar. Structural requirements: (R1) proof pressure at 1.5 x MEOP = 90 bar held for 2 minutes, max von Mises <= yield (hot) / 1.1 = 218 MPa; (R2) burst pressure 2.5 x MEOP = 150 bar with ultimate margin >= ultimate (hot) / 1.25 = 221 MPa max von Mises; (R3) the emergency depressurization burst disk port must be integral to the chamber wall and sized to vent chamber to <= 10% MEOP = 6 bar within 5 seconds following activation; (R4) dry mass of the chamber case <= 3.5 kg; (R5) external thermal boundary wall temperature must not exceed 150 C during 6.0 s nominal burn with an ablative liner of EPDM/silica-phenolic (this thermal constraint is the binding design driver, not pure structural). Verification: primary structural FEA with the hot-derated properties at proof and burst; secondary thermal analysis (transient 1D conduction through ablative liner + chamber wall) to confirm R5; vibration and buckling secondary if explicitly requested. Fluid (CFD) and radiation not required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242, materials certificate, and a report listing wall thickness in mm, dry mass in kg, max von Mises at proof and burst in MPa, emergency-vent time in s, peak external wall temperature in C during burn.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part, monolithic N2O/HTPB hybrid-rocket combustion chamber case for a student sounding rocket targeting 30,000 ft. The case is a seamless aluminum cylinder (spin-formed or machined) with two integral bolted bosses: forward injector-bulkhead interface and aft nozzle-retention interface. An ablative liner (EPDM/silica-phenolic) protects the hot side, so the metal is design-derated to a 150 C hot-wall condition.

Envelope: internal diameter 140 mm, internal length 700 mm. MEOP 60 bar chamber pressure with a 1.1 x MEOP transient spike allowance.

Load cases.
- LC1 proof: 1.5 x MEOP = 90 bar held for 2 minutes at 150 C wall.
- LC2 burst: 2.5 x MEOP = 150 bar at 150 C wall.
- LC3 emergency depressurization: integral burst-disk port must vent chamber to <= 10% MEOP within 5 s.
- LC4 thermal: nominal 6.0 s burn, external wall temperature must stay <= 150 C with the ablative liner (this is the binding driver).

prompt.title: Student-developed hybrid combustion chamber (30 kft apogee class)
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic hybrid chamber, 60 bar MEOP, N2O oxidizer, 6 s nominal burn. Must pass 1.5x proof, 2.5x burst, emergency burst-disk integration, and wall temp <= 150 C.

load_cases.name: emergency_vent
load_cases.description: vent from MEOP to <=10% MEOP within 5 s

boundary_conditions.thermal: convection + ablative liner on inner wall, free convection outside

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Chamber internal combustion CFD is out of scope; gas-side boundary is reduced to uniform pressure + convective film coefficient.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Radiative load absorbed into film coefficient on inner wall.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Pressurized shell; no external crush load.
